content.content:
Recipe Bottle — Onboarding Start

  ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#\Recipe Bottle builds container images with supply-chain provenance
  and runs untrusted containers behind enforced network isolation.

  This menu points you at handbook tracks — self-describing teaching
  documents that explain concepts and show you live probe status.

Foundation

    Configure your Repo's Environment
      Universal prerequisite. ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tabtarget\Tabtargets, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Regime\Regimes,
      ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#BURS\BURS setup, validation, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Log\Logs. Local-only, no cloud.
        tt/rbw-Occ.OnboardingConfigureEnvironment.sh

    Install ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Retriever\Retriever Credentials
      For joining an established project.
      Place your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRA\RBRA credential file, verify, confirm you can pull images.
        tt/rbw-Ocr.OnboardingCredentialRetriever.sh

    Install ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Director\Director Credentials
      For joining an established project.
      Place your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRA\RBRA credential file, verify, confirm you can build and publish.
        tt/rbw-Ocd.OnboardingCredentialDirector.sh

Kludged Crucibles

  Two tracks that locally build the images for a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible and then start it.
  Both share the same mechanical middle:

    * Build images locally — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry/]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pentacle\Pentacle and ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle
    * Start the sandbox    — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible

  They diverge on what happens once the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible is ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charged:

    Inhabit the sandbox — explorer track
      The ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#ccyolo\ccyolo ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible runs Claude Code in a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle that
      can only reach Anthropic. Requires a Claude OAuth subscription.
      After ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge:
        * Shell into the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle   — SSH in, run Claude Code
        * Verify network containment — manual curl against the allowlist
        tt/rbw-Ofc.OnboardingFirstCrucible.sh

    Prove containment under attack — evaluator track
      The tadmor ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible pairs the same ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry with a hostile
      ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle carrying the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ifrit\Ifrit attack binary; >30 authored cases
      exercise the containment primitives.
      After ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge:
        * Tour the architecture      — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry/]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pentacle\Pentacle/]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle layers, defense-in-depth
        * Run the adversarial suite  — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Theurge\Theurge + ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ifrit\Ifrit, per-case result reading
        tt/rbw-Ots.OnboardingTadmorSecurity.sh

Create Payor and Depot

  A ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot is the facility where the team's container images are
  built and stored — the ground truth other tracks rest on.

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Payor\Payor — establish a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Manor\Manor and provision the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot
        tt/rbw-Op.OnboardingPayor.sh

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Governor\Governor — administer service accounts for ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Director\Directors and ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Retriever\Retrievers
        tt/rbw-Og.OnboardingGovernor.sh

Director Subtracks

    Your First Cloud Build
      Provision the builder toolchain, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain your first
      ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel via Cloud Build, and tour the result.
      Steps:
        * Inscribe the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Reliquary\Reliquary — provision builder tool images
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry — first tethered cloud build
        * Tour: ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tally\Tally, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Plumb\Plumb, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pouch\Pouch — inspect images and SLSA
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Summon\Summon — pull the hallmark locally
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Abjure\Abjure and ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Rekon\Rekon — hallmark lifecycle
      Requires: Director credentials and a provisioned Depot.
        tt/rbw-Odf.OnboardingDirectorFirstBuild.sh

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Airgap\Airgap Cloud Build
      Build a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle with zero external network during Cloud Build.
      Pre-stage every input in the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot first — rust upstream base,
      then a project-authored toolchain image (the forge) — then build the
      airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle against those pre-staged layers.
      Steps:
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine the rust upstream — mirror base into the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered, then re-]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine — toolchain image as new base
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Airgap\Airgap — zero-network build from the forge
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge moriah and run the adversarial suite against the airgap-built ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle
        * Compare ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Plumb\Plumb — airgap vs ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance side by side
      Requires: Director credentials, a provisioned Depot, and Your First Cloud Build completed.
        tt/rbw-Oda.OnboardingDirectorAirgap.sh

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bind\Bind — Safe PlantUML Container
      Mirror an upstream image by digest — no Dockerfile, no build.
      PlantUML renders diagrams but its Docker Hub image could
      phone home. Bind pins it; the Sentry blocks all egress.
      Steps:
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bind\Bind ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bind\PlantUML — pin upstream image by digest
        * Inspect ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch verdict — digest-pin, no SLSA (image not built here)
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\pluml ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible — render a diagram, observe blocked egress
      You get the tool without the risk.

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft — Local Image Publishing
      Push a locally-built image to the Depot. The user owns the
      entire build — SLSA cannot vouch for this image. Vouch verdict
      is GRAFTED: an explicit signal that provenance stops at the
      local machine. Not the enterprise path for safe image creation.
      Steps:
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge a vessel image locally
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft — push local image to the Depot
        * Inspect ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch verdict — GRAFTED, no provenance chain
      Development and prototyping workflow, not production supply chain.

    Inhabit the sandbox — explorer track
      The ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#ccyolo\ccyolo ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible runs Claude Code in a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle that
      can only reach Anthropic. Requires a Claude OAuth subscription.
      After ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge:
        * Shell into the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle   — SSH in, run Claude Code
        * Verify network containment — manual curl against the allowlist
        tt/rbw-Ofc.OnboardingFirstCrucible.sh

    Prove containment under attack — evaluator track
      The tadmor ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible pairs the same ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry with a hostile
      ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle carrying the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ifrit\Ifrit attack binary; >30 authored cases
      exercise the containment primitives.
      After ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge:
        * Tour the architecture      — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry/]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pentacle\Pentacle/]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle layers, defense-in-depth
        * Run the adversarial suite  — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Theurge\Theurge + ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ifrit\Ifrit, per-case result reading
        tt/rbw-Ots.OnboardingTadmorSecurity.sh

Create Payor and Depot

  A ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot is the facility where the team's container images are
  built and stored — the ground truth other tracks rest on.

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Payor\Payor — establish a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Manor\Manor and provision the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot
        tt/rbw-Op.OnboardingPayor.sh

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Governor\Governor — administer service accounts for ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Director\Directors and ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Retriever\Retrievers
        tt/rbw-Og.OnboardingGovernor.sh

Director Subtracks

    Your First Cloud Build
      Provision the builder toolchain, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain your first
      ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel via Cloud Build, and tour the result.
      Steps:
        * Inscribe the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Reliquary\Reliquary — provision builder tool images
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Sentry\Sentry — first tethered cloud build
        * Tour: ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tally\Tally, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Plumb\Plumb, ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Pouch\Pouch — inspect images and SLSA
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Summon\Summon — pull the hallmark locally
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Abjure\Abjure and ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Rekon\Rekon — hallmark lifecycle
      Requires: Director credentials and a provisioned Depot.
        tt/rbw-Odf.OnboardingDirectorFirstBuild.sh

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Airgap\Airgap Cloud Build
      Build a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle with zero external network during Cloud Build.
      Pre-stage every input in the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot first — rust upstream base,
      then a project-authored toolchain image (the forge) — then build the
      airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle against those pre-staged layers.
      Steps:
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine the rust upstream — mirror base into the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered, then re-]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine — toolchain image as new base
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Airgap\Airgap — zero-network build from the forge
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge moriah and run the adversarial suite against the airgap-built ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle
        * Compare ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Plumb\Plumb — airgap vs ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance side by side
      Requires: Director credentials, a provisioned Depot, and Your First Cloud Build completed.
        tt/rbw-Oda.OnboardingDirectorAirgap.sh

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bind\Bind — Safe PlantUML Container
      Mirror an upstream image by digest — no Dockerfile, no build.
      PlantUML renders diagrams but its Docker Hub image could
      phone home. Bind pins it; the Sentry blocks all egress.
      Steps:
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bind\Bind ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bind\PlantUML — pin upstream image by digest
        * Inspect ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch verdict — digest-pin, no SLSA (image not built here)
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\pluml ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible — render a diagram, observe blocked egress
      You get the tool without the risk.

    ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft — Local Image Publishing
      Push a locally-built image to the Depot. The user owns the
      entire build — SLSA cannot vouch for this image. Vouch verdict
      is GRAFTED: an explicit signal that provenance stops at the
      local machine. Not the enterprise path for safe image creation.
      Steps:
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Kludge\Kludge a vessel image locally
        * ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Graft\Graft — push local image to the Depot
        * Inspect ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch verdict — GRAFTED, no provenance chain
      Development and prototyping workflow, not production supply chain.

content.content:
Airgap Cloud Build — Your Own Supply Chain

In Your First Cloud Build you ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordained a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel on the
]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered pool — Cloud Build pulled base images from the
public internet during the build. ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Airgap\Airgap removes that
dependency: zero network during the build, every input pre-staged
in your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot.

This track builds ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap — the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle variant that
matches the ifrit you met in the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\tadmor adversarial suite,
now with full supply-chain discipline. The chain has three links:

  1. Mirror the rust base from upstream into your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot (]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine)
  2. Build a project-authored toolchain image (the forge) ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered
  3. Build the airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Airgap\Airgap from the enshrined forge

Then drive the resulting ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark into the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\moriah
]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate and run the same 34 containment attacks against
it that you ran against ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\tadmor.

Prerequisites:

 [*] ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Director\Director credential installed
 [*] ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot configured (RBRR_DEPOT_PROJECT_ID populated)

This track assumes you have completed Your First Cloud Build —
the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Reliquary\Reliquary is inscribed and you have ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordained at least
one ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel on the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered pool.

Configure this handbook session:

   export HANDBOOK_NAMEPLATE=moriah

The ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge and suite commands below reference the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate
by name. The two ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel names shift mid-track (forge →
airgap), so vessels appear as literals in commands.

Step 1 — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine the upstream base

   The airgap chain starts with ownership. ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine mirrors
   an upstream image — here rust:slim-bookworm — into your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot
   under a content-addressed anchor. Once enshrined, builds pull the
   base from your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot without touching the public internet.

   The forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge declares its upstream base in
   its ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRV\RBRV file:

      RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=rust:slim-bookworm
      RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=rust-slim-bookworm-5ae2d2ef98

   ORIGIN names where the image comes from; ANCHOR is the name it
   takes inside your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot. Run ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine:

      tt/rbw-dE.DirectorEnshrinesVessel.sh

   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine sweeps every ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Regime\Regime, resolves the
   upstream bases they declare, and creates the mirror tags. It's
   idempotent — already-enshrined bases are skipped.

Step 2 — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered, then ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine its result

   The forge is a project-authored toolchain image. It pre-stages
   apt packages and warms the cargo cache so the airgap build
   downstream has nothing to fetch.

   Build it on the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered pool — the forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRV\RBRV declares:

      RBRV_EGRESS_MODE=tether

   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain reads this and routes to the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered pool:

      tt/rbw-fO.DirectorOrdainsHallmark.sh rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge

   Wall-clock ~15-20 minutes across the declared platforms. The
   forge is toolchain plumbing that your customer code will be
   built against — it is not itself customer code, which is why
   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered build is acceptable at this layer.

   Now bridge: the airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap declares the
   forge as its base:

      RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge
      RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=

   ANCHOR starts empty because it resolves to the forge's freshly
   built ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark. Re-run ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine to populate it:

      tt/rbw-dE.DirectorEnshrinesVessel.sh

   This time ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine sees the internal reference, finds the
   forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark in your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot, and writes the resolved
   anchor into the airgap vessel's ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRV\RBRV. Commit that change.

    [ ] Airgap base anchor empty — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the forge and re-run ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine

Step 3 — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Airgap\Airgap

   Now the airgap build has everything it needs inside your
   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot — rust toolchain, apt packages, cargo cache, all
   pre-staged in the enshrined forge.

Step 2 — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered, then ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine its result

   The forge is a project-authored toolchain image. It pre-stages
   apt packages and warms the cargo cache so the airgap build
   downstream has nothing to fetch.

   Build it on the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered pool — the forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRV\RBRV declares:

      RBRV_EGRESS_MODE=tether

   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain reads this and routes to the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered pool:

      tt/rbw-fO.DirectorOrdainsHallmark.sh rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge

   Wall-clock ~15-20 minutes across the declared platforms. The
   forge is toolchain plumbing that your customer code will be
   built against — it is not itself customer code, which is why
   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered build is acceptable at this layer.

   Now bridge: the airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap declares the
   forge as its base:

      RBRV_IMAGE_1_ORIGIN=rbev-bottle-ifrit-forge
      RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR=

   ANCHOR starts empty because it resolves to the forge's freshly
   built ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark. Re-run ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine to populate it:

      tt/rbw-dE.DirectorEnshrinesVessel.sh

   This time ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine sees the internal reference, finds the
   forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark in your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot, and writes the resolved
   anchor into the airgap vessel's ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#RBRV\RBRV. Commit that change.

    [ ] Airgap base anchor empty — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the forge and re-run ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine

Step 3 — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure the airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Airgap\Airgap

   Now the airgap build has everything it needs inside your
   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot — rust toolchain, apt packages, cargo cache, all
   pre-staged in the enshrined forge.

input.description: Render remaining lines of airgap handbook

content.content:
pre-staged in the enshrined forge.

   The airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vessel\Vessel's Dockerfile starts FROM the forge:

      ARG RBF_IMAGE_1
      FROM ${RBF_IMAGE_1}

   RBF_IMAGE_1 is resolved from RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR at build time.
   The airgap pool has zero external network — Cloud Build reaches
   your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot and nothing else.

   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain:

      tt/rbw-fO.DirectorOrdainsHallmark.sh rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap

   Another ~15-20 minutes. The ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch at the end attests both
   the SLSA ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance chain and the airgap condition — that
   the build saw no public internet.

   Your customer code was compiled against a supply chain you
   controlled end-to-end.

Step 4 — Install the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark into ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\moriah and run the security suite

   The cloud-built ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark is now in your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot. The
   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\moriah ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate names how that ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark runs as
   a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible. ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark and ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate are separate
   artifacts — one is the build product, the other is the deployment
   config.

   Read the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark from the fact file ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Ordain\Ordain wrote:

      export HANDBOOK_HALLMARK=$(cat /Users/bhyslop/projects/rbm_alpha_recipemuster/../output-buk/current/rbf_fact_hallmark)

   Open .rbk/moriah/rbrn.env and set the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle hallmark, replacing
   the PENDING-ordination placeholder:

      RBRN_BOTTLE_HALLMARK=${HANDBOOK_HALLMARK}

   Commit the change.

    [ ] ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\moriah ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle hallmark is PENDING-ordination — install it above

   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\moriah with the airgap-built image:

      tt/rbw-cC.Charge.moriah.sh

   Run the full 34-case security suite — the same suite you ran
   against ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\tadmor with kludged ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmarks:

      tt/rbtd-r.Run.moriah.sh

   Expect green across the board. Same attacks, same containment
   boundaries, same expected responses — now against a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle
   built with full airgap supply-chain discipline.

   This closes the loop: you built the validated airgap security
   infrastructure yourself. Containment holds regardless of how the
   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark was produced.

Step 5 — Compare ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Plumb\Plumb output — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered vs ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Airgap\Airgap

   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Plumb\Plumb displays the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance evidence attached to a
   ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark. Run it against ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\moriah's airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle
   alongside ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\tadmor's ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle to see what
   airgap adds to the record.

    [*] ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\tadmor bottle hallmark available for comparison

   Airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle full ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance:

      tt/rbw-fpf.RetrieverPlumbsFull.sh rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap ${HANDBOOK_HALLMARK}

   Tethered ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle — ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\tadmor's current bottle ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark is k260417074933-cf716423:

      tt/rbw-fpf.RetrieverPlumbsFull.sh rbev-bottle-ifrit-tether k260417074933-cf716423

   What to look for, side by side:

     ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#SBOM\SBOM       Same rust/cargo package set if both ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmarks
                 were ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjured. The ifrit binary is functionally
                 equivalent either way.

     Build info  The airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark records a sealed supply
                 chain — base digests resolved to your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot,
                 no upstream resolution during the build. A ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered
                 ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark shows upstream resolution within the build
                 window. A kludged ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmark carries no Cloud Build
                 record at all.

     ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance  The airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouch attests the airgap condition
                 in addition to the standard SLSA chain. ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered
                 ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Vouch\Vouched ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Hallmark\Hallmarks carry SLSA without that
                 extra attestation.

   For a compact summary:

      tt/rbw-fpc.RetrieverPlumbsCompact.sh rbev-bottle-ifrit-airgap ${HANDBOOK_HALLMARK}

What you learned

You built an airgap supply chain end to end:

  1. ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Enshrine\Enshrine — rust upstream base mirrored into your ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Depot\Depot
  2. Forge ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure — project toolchain image built ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered
  3. Airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Conjure\Conjure — customer code built with zero external network
  4. ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Nameplate\Nameplate install + ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Charge\Charge — runtime config assembled, suite green
  5. ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Plumb\Plumb comparison — airgap ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance visible against the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Tethered\Tethered baseline

The same 34 containment attacks pass against a ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle you
built with discipline over your entire supply chain. The attack
surface is the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Crucible\Crucible; the build ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Provenance\Provenance is separate
evidence about how the ]8;;https://github.com/bhyslop/recipemuster/blob/main/README.md#Bottle\Bottle came to exist.

Return to start: tt/rbw-o.ONBOARDING.sh

Two hard-gate probes (director credential, depot) mirror rbhodf's prerequisite block with early-return on failure; three dynamic probes render inline (airgap anchor populated via RBRV_IMAGE_1_ANCHOR, moriah bottle hallmark installed vs PENDING-ordination, tadmor tether hallmark available for comparison) so the handbook is self-describing regardless of where the learner is in the track. Nameplate-facing interpolation uses HANDBOOK_NAMEPLATE env var following rbhots; vessel names are bash-substituted (forge/airgap) since they shift mid-track — explicitly documented in the opening configure-your-session block. Extensive RBYC_* linked-term usage throughout: ORDAIN/CONJURE/ENSHRINE/VOUCH/PLUMB operations, VESSEL/HALLMARK/DEPOT/RELIQUARY/NAMEPLATE/BOTTLE artifacts, TETHERED/AIRGAP modes, CRUCIBLE/SENTRY/PENTACLE components, DIRECTOR/PAYOR roles, PROBE_YES/NO/SBOM/PROVENANCE/HALLMARKS/VOUCHED/CONJURED variants. Onboarding menu wired: the stale Airgap Cloud Build placeholder in rbho0_start_here.sh Director Subtracks (no tabtarget link, 'same vessel, full isolation' copy that described a sentry-rebuild rather than the forge+ifrit chain) replaced with a parallel-to-Odf entry whose 5 bullet steps mirror the teaching arc and whose Requires line names 'Your First Cloud Build completed' as the intellectual prerequisite. Single quality fix during review: dropped an unused z_lk_tether_v link capture that was declared but never consumed (z_tether_vessel itself is still referenced bare in the plumb comparison command).
